text.title: The Real Bali Most People Miss | 4K Travel Documentary
text.u: planning a trip to Barley can feel
overwhelming with so many incredible
places to visit and experiences to
choose
from but what many Travelers don't
realize is that there's a way to see the
best of barley without missing out on
its hidden
gems in this video I'll guide you
through the most beautiful and iconic
destinations in barley ensuring you
don't miss a single highlight on your
journey first I will tell you some
interesting facts about barley and then
we'll dive into the best places to visit
in this captivating
destination did you know barley is more
than just a single
Island this province in Indonesia
includes three stunning Islands the
famous nusa penida Nua lembongan and
nusa
chenan barley is also the only Hindu
majority area in Indonesia creating a
unique spiritual Vibe that's truly one
of a kind
one of Barley's Most Fascinating
Traditions is Nei A Day of Silence and
reflection when the entire Island shuts
down yes even the airport
closes this annual event allows everyone
to pause reflect and embrace the peace
and
quiet and for all you coffee lovers out
there Bary is home to the world's most
expensive coffee copi
luak this unique Brew is made through a
special process involving civet cats
making it a must try for coffee
afficionados
but that's not all Barley's culture is
distinct with its own language dance
forms music and rituals it's a cultural
Treasure Trove that's unlike anywhere
else in
Indonesia nature enthusiasts will be
thrilled to know that barley is home to
several active volcanoes including Mount
aong and mount
B these Peaks offer adventurous hiking
opportunities and stunning views that
are sure to take your breath
away adding to Barley's charm is its
thriving expat Community especially in
hotpots like kangu and
Ubud people from all over the world are
drawn to Barley's vibrant culture and
lifestyle creating a dynamic and diverse
environment so whether you're into
spiritual Retreats cultural experiences
or outdoor adventure Ventures barley has
something special waiting for
you now let's dive into the best places
to visit in barley and discover what
makes each spot so
magical start your barley Adventure at
tegalalang Rice Terraces stunning rice
patties carved into Rolling
Hills these emerald green fields are
famous for Sunrise views and photo
opportunities imagine waking up to the
Sun Painting The Rice Terraces a golden
color
beautiful tagala Lang isn't just pretty
it's also historic The Rice Terraces are
a clever way to farm on slopes farmers
use a special irrigation system called
subac to bring water to all the levels
this system is so impressive it's a
UNESCO world heritage
site take a walk through the Rice
Terraces and learn from local farmers
how they grow the rice you might even
get a chance to take a photo on a cool
swing overlooking the whole
scene tal laang shows Barley's farming
routes and the connection between people
people and nature on this amazing
Island just a short drive away we arrive
at a really cool place the ubed monkey
Forest it's a protected area for over
700 monkeys with long tails called
Macs there are also three very old
temples there daram aung padangal holy
spring Temple and prajapati Temple the
whole place is like a big Park about the
size of 27 football fields and it's full
of plants and trees over 186 different
kinds the interesting thing about this
place is that you can see the monkeys
hanging out in their natural environment
playing around and just being monkeys
you can also explore the temples which
are important historical and religious
sites the monkeys are kind of used to
people being around so they might come
up close to check you out which can be
pretty fun it's like a magical place
where nature and religion come together
right in the center of Ubud you'll find
a stunning water temple named Pura Taman
Kuda
Saraswati this place is dedicated to
Sarah Swati a goddess well known in
Hinduism for being in charge of
knowledge and the
Arts the temple itself is amazing with
all sorts of detailed carvings
but the real showstopper is the giant
Lotus Pond in front making the whole
place super
peaceful you can even catch traditional
balines dances happening in the temple
Courtyard if you're not into dancing
that's okay too just wander around the
grounds and soak up the positive vibes
take a short trip North and you'll find
TI empul
Temple this place is famous for its holy
spring water which people have been
using for purification for over a
thousand years
think of it as a special washing place
for the
soul the temple itself is really old
built way back in 962 ad it's got
amazing architecture and a super calm
feeling surrounded by lots of plants
walking around the temple grounds you
learn how important religious practices
and water are to the religion of Balan
Hinduism
the coolest part this Temple is still in
use today you might even see locals
doing their traditional ceremonies
giving you a peak into Barley's deep
culture and religious Traditions it's a
powerful experience for anyone who
visits with beautiful scenery and a
spiritual
atmosphere penglipuran Village is like a
trip back in time to Barley's
traditional past
this Village is famous for keeping its
old ways alive from the way their houses
are built to how they live their
everyday
lives the village is very clean and
organized and everyone seems to get
along really
well you can walk down their neat
streets paved with stones and see the
cool traditional
houses even better you can chat with the
friendly locals and learn learn all
about their customs and daily
routines it's like stepping into a real
life postcard of Barley's rural
life because they've done such a good
job of keeping their Traditions alive
penglipuran has become famous for its
culture it's one of the cleanest and
most interesting villages in barley
making it a perfect place to experience
the true Spirit of barley and learn
about their
Heritage you you might even get a chance
to join in on a local ceremony and feel
the welcoming atmosphere of Baran
Hospitality imagine a giant beautiful
Balan gate Standing Tall in front of
Rolling Green Hills and Misty
Mountains that's the hondara gate it's
actually the entrance to a golf course
but it's become very famous because of
how amazing it
looks the gate itself is decorated with
cool carvings and has a unique split
design it's a perfect spot to snap a
photo that captures the beauty of
Barley's nature and culture all in one
go the scenery around the gate is
peaceful and calming and the way the
gate is built makes it look like a piece
of
art even though it's just a gate it
stands for the spiritual and artistic
side of barley with its mix of natural
beauty and cool architecture guaranteed
you'll be impressed by this dramatic
gate against the lush green
background just outside Ubud you'll find
tegenungan waterfall a beautiful spot
known for its cool refreshing water and
Lush
surroundings imagine a waterfall
cascading down into a calm pool
surrounded by tropical plants perfect
for a swim or just
relaxing you can either take in the view
from a platform at the top or hike down
for a closer look the waterfall's power
is pretty
amazing this place is a great way to
escape the crowds and enjoy Barley's
natural beauty plus it's close to ubed
so it's an easy trip there are even
cafes nearby where you can grab a drink
and soak up the
scenery Central barley boasts a must do
for any thrill seeker Mount
batter this active volcano isn't just a
fiery
giant it's a favorite spot for hikers
looking for a breathtaking Sunrise
experience imagine this you start your
Trek in the pre-dawn hours climbing
towards the summit as the sky begins to
lighten by the time you reach the top
roughly 2 hours later you're rewarded
with a panoramic Sunrise view that will
leave you
speechless below you stretches out a
landscape of volcanic Majesty including
the beautiful Lake batur nestled within
the
Caldera the hike itself is considered
moderately challenging perfect for those
with a decent Fitness
level as you make your way up you'll get
to experience the unique volcanic
terrain feeling the cool Mountain Air
invigorate you reaching the summit isn't
just about the view it's a sense of
accomplishment you won't forget
heading east we arrive at TI
ganga a Royal Water Palace with
enchanting Gardens and inviting
pools built in 1948 by the last king of
karangasem it feels like a giant Garden
Party Frozen in
Time Lush Greenery surrounds the place
with teered fountains cool Stone C
carings and statues scattered around the
best part there are swimming pools fed
by natural springs imagine wandering
through winding Pathways stepping across
Stones over water and perhaps even
taking a dip in the refreshing
pools the name itself Tita ganga
translates to holy water from the Ganges
hinting at the Spirit spiritual
significance of this
place it's a peaceful spot to relax and
reflect while also learning a bit about
Barley's Royal history and the Banes
reverence for water as a source of life
and
purification nearby we have another
stunning Water Palace called Taman
sasada uong
this one was built in 1909 by a
different king of
karangasem Imagine a place that mixes
Balan Style with European flare that's
exactly what this Palace is
like the whole place is huge with big
Gardens pools you can't swim in but are
still pretty and elegant Bridges
connecting everything
thing you can walk around the perfectly
manicured Lawns take in the amazing
views of the ocean and mountains and
just soak up the peaceful
atmosphere this Palace is a great
example of how skilled Balan Builders
were and how fancy Kings used to live
it's a tranquil escape and a window into
Barley's Royal past
gunung kawi Temple is like an open air
museum carved into a
cliff built over a thousand years ago it
has 10 giant shrines each 7 m tall
carved right into the rock
face these shrines are most likely tombs
for Barley's ancient kings and
queens the temple sits in a beautiful
spot surrounded by Green rice fields and
a peaceful River
you can walk around and see the
impressive carvings up
close it's a quiet place perfect for
soaking up the
atmosphere gunung kawi isn't just old
it's a reminder of Barley's long history
its religious beliefs and its unique
culture want to explore a hidden temple
and escape the crowds
purong seang is your
spot this peaceful Temple is tucked away
in the countryside perfect for those who
want to experience the spiritual side of
barley without the hustle and
bustle dedicated to the Mountain Gods
the temple features beautiful balines
architecture and is surrounded by Lush
Greenery and Scenic
views you can wander around the grounds
soaking up the calm atmosphere even
cooler you might get to see a local
ceremony and learn what the temple means
to the people
here Pura gunung seing is a more
intimate way to experience Balan
religion and culture making it a great
place to relax and learn something
new picture a giant postcard with rice
patties like stairs climbing Green Hill
Hills that's siden
Valley this Peaceful Valley in eastern
barley is Untouched by The Busy tourist
areas small traditional Villages dot the
landscape where life goes on at a slower
Pace you might even see mount arong a
giant volcano in the
distance want to relax
hike or bike through the beautiful rice
fields explore the Charming Villages or
just enjoy the peace and
quiet feeling more adventurous learn how
to make cool woven crafts just like the
ones you see around barley at local
workshops if you're looking to escape
the crowds and experience the real
barley sidemen Valley is the place for
you
it's a quiet place where you can slow
down enjoy the beautiful scenery and
experience the true Spirit of
barley we can't miss Mount aong it's the
tallest mountain in
barley reaching a whopping 3,031 M this
active volcano is very important to the
people of barley because they believe
it's the home of the
Gods climbing to the top is a challenge
it takes a whole day of trekking through
thick forests and steep slopes but if
you make it you'll be rewarded with
Incredible views of the entire Island
and even Beyond because of its religious
significance and amazing Beauty Mount
arong is a mustsee for both people who
want a spiritual experience and those
who love Adventure it's a tough climb
but totally worth it
imagine a beautiful Ancient Temple in
the mountains of
barley this is lempuyang temple one of
the oldest and most important holy
places on the
island it's built on Seven Levels with
smaller shrines leading up to the main
one at the very
top you can wander around the peaceful
grounds take part in local ceremonies
and enjoy amazing views
the coolest part there's a special gate
called the Gates of
Heaven that frames a stunning view of
Mount aung
volcano it's especially beautiful at
Sunrise and makes for incredible
pictures imagine a giant beautiful
Temple complex nestled on the side of a
volcano in
barley that's Baki Temple the most
important Hindu temple on the whole
island people call it the Mother Temple
because it's so special basaki isn't
just one Temple it's actually a bunch of
temples 23 to be exact built on
different levels like a giant staircase
each part is like a mini Temple
decorated and dedicated to different
different gods and
goddesses the views from Baki are
amazing you can see all of B's Beauty
from up there it's also a really
interesting place to learn about ban
culture which is all about
Hinduism Baki is incredibly old likely
dating back thousands of years its
importance as a holy sight stretches
back to prehistoric times with some
Temple bases resembling structures from
at least 2,000 years
ago and even though the volcano next to
it erupted in
1963 basak wasn't badly damaged this
only made it more sacred to the balane
people Padang Bai is the perfect place
to wind down after your Eastern
Adventure This Charming Coastal Village
boasts beautiful beaches and amazing
diving
spots it's a ferry port for other
Islands but don't let that fool you
Padang Bai is worth exploring on its
own imagine relaxing on the pristine
Sands of Blue Lagoon beach the crystal
clear water teeming with colorful fish
just waiting to be seen while you
snorkel or for the more adventurous
diving trips can whisk you away to
vibrant coral reefs and hidden under
water
caves Padang Bai isn't just about the
water though local eateries and markets
tempt your taste buds with fresh seafood
and traditional Balinese Cuisine The
Village itself has a laid-back
atmosphere perfect for soaking up the
natural beauty and unwinding before you
head home
up north in barley you'll find a whole
different Rice Terrace experience at
jalu unlike the steeper busier
tegalalang Terraces jtil is huge over
600 hectares that means Rolling Green
Hills stretching as far as the eye can
see perfect for peaceful walks or even a
bike
ride just like teal
jalou is a UNESCO world heritage site
thanks to the amazing soac irrigation
system here you can really see how
clever it is bringing water to all the
rice
fields plus the views are incredible
especially at sunrise and sunset when
the fields glow
golden joui is also less crowded than
tal laang so you can soak up the scenery
and appreciate the connection between
the balane people and their land
it's a more peaceful way to experience
Barley's farming
Heritage so while teal laang offers
those dramatic slopes and close
encounters with Farmers jatila is for
those who want to spread out and enjoy
the vast beauty of Barley's Rice
Terraces both places show off the
amazing skills of baranes farmers but
jatal Loui lets you relax and take it
all in
imagine a majestic Temple seemingly
floating on a lake surrounded by cool
mountains that's Pura ulun danu bratan a
breathtaking site nestled in bedugul
North
Bley this iconic Temple complex sits on
the shores of Lake Braton and during
rainy Seasons the water level rises
creating the illusion that the temple
itself is floating
dedicated to De danu the goddess of
water Pura ulun danu Braton holds a
special place in the hearts of Balinese
people it's a key site for ceremonies
related to water and agriculture
reflecting how important these elements
are to Barley's
lifeblood as you explore you'll be
greeted by beautifully manicured Gardens
intricate architecture showcasing Balan
Artistry and the chance to take a
peaceful boat ride on the
lake the Serene atmosphere here is truly
special the reflection of the temple on
the glassy water creates a magical scene
making Pura ulun danu bratan a must sea
for anyone visiting Northern
barley Northern barley is a dream
destination for waterfall lovers
offering a mix of peace excitement and
natural
beauty from Jungle hikes to fun water
slides these waterfalls promise an e
forgettable
experience start with cumple waterfall
often called Barley's most
beautiful a walk through Lush jungles
past rice fields and small villages
leads you to this stunning spot
here seven waterfalls plunge into Clear
Pools dive into the cool water and enjoy
the soothing sounds of the
falls for those who love Adventure aing
aing waterfall is perfect this 35 M
waterfall flows into a deep pool but the
real fun is in the natural water slides
and jumping spots
nearby guided tours make sure you're
safe and show the best places to slide
and jump combining excitement with
beauty if you're looking for peace and
quiet head to banala Waterfall this twin
waterfall is hidden in a calm Valley
surrounded by a lush rainforest the
clear pool at the bottom is a perfect
place to swim relax and let the calming
sounds of the falls wash away your
stress
further north you'll find the hidden
treasure of Fiji waterfall in luki
Village this spot has three waterfalls
flowing down Green
Cliffs the hike to this remote spot is
an adventure itself with cool air and
beautiful
views once there you can swim in the
refreshing water and enjoy the peace
away from the crowds
imagine a peaceful spot tucked away in
Barley's cool mountains next to a
beautiful
Lake that's Pura ulun danu tambling
Gan this old Temple sits by tambling and
Lake surrounded by thick forests and
mountains shrouded in Mist it's a
magical and calming
place a remote gem compared to Barley's
famous beaches and tourist centers in
the
South this means it's quieter and offers
a more authentic experience a lesser
known treasure for those seeking
something
different the temple is one of many
built by the lake to honor de danu the
lake
goddess you can wander around the temple
grounds soak up the peacefulness of the
lake and even be part of some
traditional bar
ceremonies because it's so Serene and
spiritual Pura uland danu tambling gun
is perfect if you're looking for a place
to relax reflect and learn more about
Balan
culture wrap up your Northern barley
trip with a stop at menjangan
Island a small island known for its
amazing underwater world
it's part of West barley National Park
and a favorite spot for snorkeling and
Diving imagine swimming in crystal clear
water surrounded by colorful coral reefs
and all sorts of fish you might even see
a sea
turtle guided tours can take you
exploring underwater or you can just
chill on the beautiful
beaches menjangan island is gorgeous
above water and Below making it a
perfect place for nature lovers and the
ideal way to end your Northern barley
Adventure the first stop on our trip
South is the Garuda wisu kenana cultural
park it's like a giant theme park based
on Hindu
gods the main attraction is a huge
statue of Vishnu riding Garuda a
mythical
bird this incredible statue is one of
the tallest in the world reaching a
whopping 120 M
High the park itself is massive covering
over 60 hectares it's like a One-Stop
shop to learn about Barley's culture and
history you can see cool exhibits watch
traditional balines dances and listen to
music there are also beautiful gardens
around the statue basically gwk is a
great place to get a taste of everything
that makes barley special if you're
exploring the island you definitely
shouldn't miss
it leaving the giant statue park behind
our next stop is uluwatu
Temple imagine a beautiful Temple
clinging to a cliff Edge overlooking the
crashing waves of the Indian
ocean that's uluu this isn't just any
Temple it's one of the most important
temples in
barley believed to keep evil spirits
away from the
island the views from the cliffs are
amazing especially when the sun sets and
paints the sky with Incredible colors
here's another cool thing at uatu you
can watch a traditional dance called
kak it's a performance with a group of
men chanting a special Rhythm while
telling a story about ramayana an
ancient Indian
epic on top of that the temple grounds
are full of monkeys they're kind of like
The Unofficial mascots of the place just
be careful with your sunglasses and hats
monkeys can be cheeky
thieves tanal lot Temple is a beautiful
Temple that sits right on a big ocean
rock it's famous for amazing sunset
views and is one of the most
photographed spots on the
island dedicated to the ocean Gods the
temple is surrounded by crashing waves
making it accessible only during during
low
tide even if you can't walk out to the
temple itself you can still visit the
area around
it enjoy the breathtaking Ocean View
browse the local shops for souvenirs and
witness the temple transform into a
magical silhouette against the setting
Sky tanal lot is a mustsee on any barley
trip
our journey through the NSA Islands
begins on nusa penida where all
inspiring Landscapes meet Picture
Perfect beaches straight out of a
dream this island is a Haven for both
nature enthusiasts and Thrill Seekers
alike start your adventure at keing King
Beach famous for its Majestic Cliffs
resembling a
T-Rex prepare for a bit of a workout as
you descend a long
staircase but the payoff a secluded
beach with pristine white sand and
crystal clear waters is well worth the
effort continue your exploration to
discover Angel's B and broken
Beach Angel's bbong is a natural pool
carved into the
Cliffside ideal for a refreshing swim
during low
tide however beware of strong waves
crashing through the Rocks the real
magic unfolds s during high tide when
the pool transforms into a mesmerizing
Infinity like Oasis creating a surreal
illusion of endless water stretching
towards the
horizon just a short distance away lies
broken Beach also known as Pari
ug here erosion has sculpted a dramatic
Archway in The Cliff
face during high tide waves crash
dramatically through through the arch
showcasing the raw power of the ocean at
low tide a hidden pathway beneath the
arch reveals a secluded beach offering a
serene escape and a true Hidden Gem
waiting to be
discovered for those seeking a secluded
Paradise venture to gamat
Bay a Scenic Trek down a path leads you
to clear waters teeming with vibrant
fish and Lively coral reefs a perfect
setting for snorkeling and unwinding in
Nature's
Tranquility next dive into the magic of
manta point a world-renowned spot for
diving and
snorkeling here graceful manta rays
glide through the water drawn by the
rich currents and cleaning stations
imagine swimming alongside these gentle
Giants a breathtaking encounter you
won't forget wrap up your adventure with
a thrilling hike through rugged terrain
to reach ATU
Beach Marvel at its unique rock
formations including a limestone
Peninsula connected by a Natural
Bridge unlike other beaches atur offers
a peaceful atmosphere with calm
Waters Perfect for relaxing and soaking
in the sun after an exhilarating hike
NSA Pena's unspoiled Beauty from its
dramatic Cliffs to its hidden CES
teeming with life makes it an essential
stop on any barley
itinerary we then visit nusa lembongan
where you can unwind on beautiful
beaches and swim in clear
waters this smaller island is perfect
for relaxation and water activities
dream Beach and Sandy Bay are great for
sunbathing and swimming while mushroom
Bay's calm waters are perfect for
snorkeling and
paddleboarding nusa lembongan is also
famous for its vibrant coral reefs
attracting divers and Marine
enthusiasts for those seeking Adventure
the Devil's tear offers stunning views
of powerful waves crashing against the
cliffs
did you notice those green
patches they are the seaweed Farms that
have been a Cornerstone of nusa
lembongan economy for
decades these Farms Produce top quality
seaweed used in products like cosmetics
and food additives providing a glimpse
into the Island's traditional way of
life and sustainable practices
our journey continues to nusa
singan connected to nusa lembongan by a
picturesque yellow suspension
bridge this smallest of the three NSA
Islands offers a blend of Adventure and
Tranquility nusa sening Gan is famous
for its stunning Blue
Lagoon perfect for cliff jumping and
enjoying the turquoise
Waters it also features beautiful
beaches like secret Beach ideal for a
peaceful
Retreat together nusa lembongan and nusa
senan offer a perfect mix of relaxation
and Adventure making them ideal
destinations for experiencing the
natural beauty and charm of Barley's
lesser known
Islands thanks for joining us on this
captivating journey through barley from
the iconic Teala Lang Rice Terraces to
the Serene beaches of nusa lembongan and
the Majestic temples like uluwatu and
Baki we hope you're inspired to explore
this enchanting Island if you enjoyed
this journey with us don't forget to hit
that like button check out other amazing
travel videos on our Channel And
subscribe for
more discover the world explore the
extraordinary see you in the next
adventure
